content:
Member-only story

The unglamorous part of shipping that nobody warns you about, and the point where I finally got tired of paying it.

8 min read
2 hours ago

--

It’s close to midnight, and I’m staring at a Stripe webhook that refuses to fire. Not because the logic is wrong. It’s the signature verification. It’s always the signature verification. I know this because I’ve been here before, on a different project, with a different domain name, solving the exact same problem the exact same way.
I check the logs. I check the raw request body against what Stripe says it sent. I already know, before I check, that the fix is going to be one line: recomputing the signature in the right format, before the body gets touched by anything else. I’ve fixed this exact bug at least three times now. Each time costs me another forty minutes I don’t get back, and none of those forty minutes taught me a single new thing.
🔓Non-member can read here
For a long time I assumed that was just what building software looked like. You hit a wall, you climb over it, and you learn something on the way down the other side. Then somewhere around the fourth or fifth project, I noticed I wasn’t learning anything anymore. I was just doing the…
